idioms.metaphor: It is very expensive / Beyond my budget
idioms.context: Used when something costs more than expected or when one's financial situation is tight.
Refers to something that exists only in imagination or is impossible to achieve.

idioms.metaphor: Repeating the same thing over and over
idioms.context: Used when someone talks about the same topic repeatedly or does the same redundant task.
Used when someone survives a very dangerous situation or big trouble.

idioms.english_literal: Will a tiger's offspring be a cat?
idioms.metaphor: Like father, like son / Greatness is inherited
idioms.context: Used when children show the same talent or strength as their parents.

idioms.english_literal: A book-drawn bottle gourd won't serve for curry
idioms.context: Used when something looks good on paper but doesn't work in reality.

idioms.english_literal: Does a donkey know the scent of camphor?
idioms.context: Used when someone cannot appreciate the value of something fine or sacred.

idioms.english_literal: The value of shade is known in the sun
idioms.metaphor: Appreciating something only after losing it
idioms.context: Used to teach the importance of valuing people or things while they are present.
